hadiths.chapter: 57. CHAPTER ON THE LIABILITY OF A WET NURSE IF SHE
hadiths.ar:
5364   -  وَّ رَّوَّى هِس َّامُّ ن ْن ُّ سَّالِمٍ عَّن ْ سُّلَّي ْمَّان َّ ن ْن ِ ج َّالِذٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إلل َّهِ ع ق َّالَّ: سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ إسْي َّا ْ ج َّزَّ ط ِن ْزإً  
ق َّا َّعْطَّاةُّ وَّلَّذَّةُّ ق َّكَّان َّ عِن ْذَّهَّا ق َّان ْطَّلَّق َّث ِ إلط ِّن ْزُّ ق َّاسْي َّا ْج َّزَّب ْ ط ِن ْزإً إ ُّج ْزَّى ق َّع َّان َّث ِ إلط ِّن ْزُّ  ن ِالْوَّلَّذِ ق َّلَّا ي ُّذْرَّى مَّا صُّن ِغَّ ن ِهِ وَّ 
إلط ِّن ْزُّ لَّأ ي ُّكَّاق َّى ق َّالَّ إلذِّي َّه ُّ كَّامِلَّه ً.  
وَّ رَّوَّإةُّ عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ إلن  ُّعْمَّان ِ عَّن ِ إن ْن ِ مُّسْكَّان َّ  عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إلل َّهِ ع  مِي ْلَّهُّ - وَّ رَّوَّإةُّ جَّم َّاذ  عَّن ِ إلْحَّلَّن ِى ِّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إلل َّهِ ع  مِي ْلَّهُّ.
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Yahya ibn Imran Al -Ash'ari narrated from 
Muhammad ibn Najiyah, who narrated from Muhammad ibn Ali, who narrated from 
Abd Al-Rahman ibn Salim, who narrated from his father, who narrated from Abu Jafar 
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}, who said:  
"Any wet nurse of a people who accidentally kills their child while sleeping - by rolling 
over and causing the child's death - the blood money is to be paid from her personal 
wealth if she took on nursing for the sake of pride and honor.  
However, if she undertook nursing due to poverty, then the blood money is upon her 
family ('Aqilah)." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.370 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.160 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.10 
p.222 • Awali Al-La’ali, Vol.3 p.615 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.859 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.265 • Bihar Al-Anwar, 
Vol.101 p.393  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 57, H. 5363
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE LIABILITY OF A WET NURSE IF SHE
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON THE LIABILITY OF A WET NURSE IF SHE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٧، ح ٥٣٦٣

hadiths.chapter: 57. CHAPTER ON THE LIABILITY OF A WET NURSE IF SHE
hadiths.ar:
5365   -  وَّ رَّوَّى جَّم َّاذ  عَّن ِ إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى ِّ ق َّالَّ: سُّن ِلَّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ إِسْي َّا ْج َّزَّ ط ِن ْزإً  ق َّذَّق َّغَّ إ ِلَّي ْهَّا وَّلَّذَّةُّ 
ق َّع َّان َّث ْ عَّن ْهُّ ن ِهِ سِن ِي ن َّ ي ُّم َّ ج َّاءَّب ْ ن ِالْوَّلَّذِ ق َّز َّعَّمَّث ْ إ ُّم ُّهُّ إ َّن  َّهَّا لأَّ ي َّعْزِق ُّهُّ   ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Hisham ibn Salim narrated from Sulayman ibn Khalid, who narrated from Abu 
Abdullah {a.s}, who said: I asked Imam {a.s} about a man who hired a wet nurse and gave 
her his child. While the child was with her, the wet nurse went and hired another wet 
nurse.  
The second wet nurse disappeared with the child, and it was unknown what happened 
to the child, and the original wet nurse could not be held accountable.  
Imam {a.s} said: “The full blood money (diyyah) is due." 
[AL SADUQ] 
This narration was also reported by Ali ibn Al -Numan from Ibn Muskan, from Abu 
Abdullah {a.s}, with the same wording. It was likewise narrated by Hammad from Al -
Halabi, from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, with the same content. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.161  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 57, H. 5364
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE LIABILITY OF A WET NURSE IF SHE
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON THE LIABILITY OF A WET NURSE IF SHE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٧، ح ٥٣٦٤

hadiths.chapter: 57. CHAPTER ON THE LIABILITY OF A WET NURSE IF SHE
hadiths.en:
Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi, who said:  
Abu Abdullah {a.s} was asked about a man who hired a wet nurse and entrusted his child 
to her. She disappeared with the child for several years and then returned with the 
child. The mother claimed that she did not recognize the child.  
Imam {a.s} said: “They do not have the right to reject him; they must accept him, for the 
wet nurse is trusted." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.161

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 57, H. 5365
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE LIABILITY OF A WET NURSE IF SHE
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON THE LIABILITY OF A WET NURSE IF SHE

hadiths.chapter: 58. CHAPTER ON WHAT LIABILITY IS OBLIGATORY UPON
hadiths.en:
Al-Husayn ibn Alwan narrated from Amr ibn Khalid, from Zayd ibn Ali, from 
his forefathers (peace be upon them), from Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s}:  
"Imam {a.s} used to hold the owner of a dog liable if it bit during the day, but he would 
not hold him liable if it bit during the night. 
And if you enter the house of people with their permission and their dog bites you, they 
are liable. However, if you enter without their permission, there is no liability upon 
them." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.161

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 58, H. 5366
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT LIABILITY IS OBLIGATORY UPON
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON WHAT LIABILITY IS OBLIGATORY UPON
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٨، ح ٥٣٦٦

hadiths.chapter: 59. CHAPTER ON UMM AL-WALAD KILLING HER MASTER
hadiths.en:
Wahb ibn Wahb narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} from 
his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} that Imam {a.s} used to say:  
"If a slave woman who has borne a child ( umm Al-walad) kills her master by mistake, 
she becomes free and there is no liability upon her. However, if she intentionally kills 
him, she is to be executed for him." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.162 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.10 p.200 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.4 
p.276 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.656 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.216 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.404

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 59, H. 5367

reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON UMM AL-WALAD KILLING HER MASTER

hadiths.chapter: 60. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY UPON ONE WHO
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Al -Sakuni: "Indeed, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} ruled 
regarding a man who came with fire and set it ablaze in the house of some people, 
causing the house, its inhabitants, and their belongings to burn.  
Imam {a.s} said: ‘He must compensate for the value of the house and everything in it, 
and then he is to be executed.'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 4 p.162 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol. 10 p.231 • Wasa’il Al -
Shi’ah, Vol.28 p.315 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.279

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 60, H. 5368
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY UPON ONE WHO
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY UPON ONE WHO
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٠، ح ٥٣٦٨

hadiths.chapter: 61. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY UPON THE
hadiths.en:
Hammad narrated from Al -Halabi, who narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, that 
he was asked about a male camel ( bukhti) that became enraged, left the house, and 
killed a man. Then the brother of the man came and struck the camel with a sword, 
injuring it.  
Imam {a.s} said: “The owner of the camel is liable for the blood money, and he is entitled 
to receive the price of his camel." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.162 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.846

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 61, H. 5369
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY UPON THE
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY UPON THE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦١، ح ٥٣٦٩

hadiths.chapter: 62. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY REGARDING THE
hadiths.en:
Ali ibn Al-Hakam narrated from Aban Al-Ahmari, who narrated from Abu Basir 
Yahya ibn Abi Al -Qasim Al-Asadi, who narrated from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn 
Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}, who said: 
"When the Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) was nearing his death, Jibril {a.s} 
descended and said:  
‘O’ Messenger of Allah {SWT}, would you like to return to the world?'  
Prophet {saws} replied: ‘No, I have conveyed the messages of my Lord {AZJ}.'  
Jibril {a.s} repeated the offer. 
Prophet {saws} responded: 'No, rather [I choose] the Highest Companion.' 
Then the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family), while the Muslims 
were gathered around him, said: ‘O’ people, there is no prophet after me and no 
tradition after my tradition. Whoever claims otherwise after this, his claim and 
innovation are in the Fire, so kill him and whoever follows him, for they are in the Fire.  
O’ people, revive retribution ( qisas) and uphold the right for its rightful owner. Do not 
become divided. Submit to Allah {SWT} and be at peace so that you may be safe.' 
Allah {SWT} has decreed:  
"Surely I will prevail, I and My messengers." Indeed, Allah {SWT} is All -Powerful, 
Almighty."  
(Surah Al-Mujadilah 58:21) 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.163 • Mishkat Al -Anwar, Vol.1 p.145 • Al-Wafi, Vol.26 
p.166

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 62, H. 5370
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY REGARDING THE
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS OBLIGATORY REGARDING THE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٢، ح ٥٣٧٠

hadiths.chapter: 63. CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
hadiths.en:
Yunus ibn Abd Al -Rahman narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who narrated 
from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said: 
I asked Imam {a.s} about a thief who entered upon a woman to steal her belongings. 
After gathering the clothes, his desires overcame him, and he violated her. When her 
son stirred, the thief struck him with an axe he had with him and killed him. After 
finishing, the thief c arried the clothes and was about to leave when the woman 
attacked him with the axe and killed him. The thief’s family came the next day 
demanding retaliation for his blood. 
Abu Abdullah {a.s} said: "His guardians who sought retaliation for his blood must pay 
the blood money for the boy. The thief is liable for what he left behind - four thousand 
dirhams - for having forced himself upon her because he committed adultery. This 
amount must be taken from his wealth. As for the woman, she bears no responsibility 
for killing him because he was a thief." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.164 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.60

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 63, H. 5371
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٣، ح ٥٣٧١

hadiths.chapter: 63. CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED REGARDING A THIEF WHO OVERPOWERS A WOMAN FOR HER PRIVATE
hadiths.ar:
5373 - وَّ رَّوَّى إلْحَّسَّن ُّ ن ْن ُّ مَّحْن ُّوب ٍ عَّن ْ عَّن ْذِ إلل َّهِ ن ْن ِ سِن َّان ٍ ق َّالَّ سَّمِعْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إلل َّهِ ع ي َّق ُّول  ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ رَّإوَّذَّ إمْزَّإ َّة ً 
عَّلَّى ن َّق ْسِهَّا جَّزَّإماً ق َّزَّمَّي ْهُّ ن ِحَّح َّزٍ ق َّا َّصَّان َّث ْ مِن ْهُّ مَّق ْي َّلًا ق َّالَّ لَّي ْسَّ عَّلَّي ْهَّا س َّى ْ ء  ق ِي مَّا ن َّي ْن َّهَّا وَّ ن َّي ْن َّ إلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ق َّا ِن ْ  
ق ُّذِّمَّث ْ إ ِلَّى إ ِمَّامٍ عَّذْلٍ إ َّهْذَّرَّ ذَّمَّهُّ.
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Al-Fudayl narrated from Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha {a.s}, who 
said: I asked Imam {a.s} about a thief who entered upon a pregnant woman and killed 
the child in her womb. The woman then took a knife and stabbed him, killing him.  
Imam {a.s} said: “The blood of the thief is void (without consequence)." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.164 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.815 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.29 
p.61  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 63, H. 5372

hadiths.chapter: 63. CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED REGARDING A THIEF WHO OVERPOWERS A WOMAN FOR HER PRIVATE
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who said:  
I heard Abu Abdullah {a.s} say regarding a man who tried to seduce a woman for unlawful 
relations, and she threw a stone at him, striking him in a fatal spot and killing him.  
Imam {a.s} said: “There is nothing upon her in the matter between her and Allah {SWT}, the 
Mighty and Majestic. And if she is brought before a just Imam, he will declare his blood 
to be void." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol. 7 p.189 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 4 p.165 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 15 p.290 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.28 p.108  
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
«

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 63, H. 5373

hadiths.chapter: 63. CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED REGARDING A THIEF WHO OVERPOWERS A WOMAN FOR HER PRIVATE
hadiths.en:
Jamil ibn Darraj narrated from Zurara, who said:  
I asked Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} about a man who forces himself 
upon a woman.  
Imam {a.s} said: “He is to be killed." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol. 7 p.189 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 4 p.165 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 15 p.290 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.28 p.108

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 63, H. 5374

hadiths.chapter: 64. CHAPTER ON A WOMAN WHO BRINGS A MAN INTO
hadiths.en:
Yunus ibn Abd Al -Rahman narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who narrated 
from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said: 
I asked Imam {a.s} about a man who married a woman, and on the night of 
consummation, the woman brought in a male friend of hers and let him enter the bridal 
chamber. When the husband approached to be intimate with his wife, the friend 
suddenly attacked him, and they fought  in the house until the husband killed the 
friend. Then the woman struck the husband and killed him because of her friend.  
Imam {a.s} said: "The woman must pay the blood money for her (male) friend, and she is 
to be executed for killing her husband." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.293 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.165 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.10 
p.209 • Al-Manaqib, Vol.4 p.255 • Awali Al -La’ali, Vol.3 p.622 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.832 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, 
Vol.29 p.62 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.258 • Mustadrak Al-Wasa’il, Vol.18 p.323

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 64, H. 5375
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON A WOMAN WHO BRINGS A MAN INTO
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON A WOMAN WHO BRINGS A MAN INTO

hadiths.chapter: 65. CHAPTER ON ONE WHO DIES IN THE CROWDS OF EID,
hadiths.en:
Al-Sakuni narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Mu hammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} from his 
father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s}, who said that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} 
said: 
"Whoever dies in the crowd of Friday prayer, Eid, Arafah, at a well, or on a bridge, and 
it is unknown who caused his death, his blood money is to be paid from the Public 
Treasury (Bayt Al-Mal)." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.165 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.833

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 65, H. 5376

hadiths.chapter: 66. CHAPTER ON A MAN WHO IS KILLED AND FOUND IN
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Sinan narrated from Talhah ibn Zayd, from Al -Fadl ibn 
Uthman, who narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s} regarding a man who was killed, and his 
head was found in one tribe, his torso and arms in another tribe, and the rest of his 
body in yet another tribe.  
Imam {a.s} said: "The blood money is upon the tribe where his torso and arms were 
found, and the funeral prayer is to be performed over him."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.1 p.167 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.166 • Al-Wafi, 
Vol.24 p.491 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.3 p.135  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 66, H. 5377
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON A MAN WHO IS KILLED AND FOUND IN
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON A MAN WHO IS KILLED AND FOUND IN
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٦، ح ٥٣٧٧

hadiths.chapter: 66. CHAPTER ON A MAN WHO IS KILLED AND FOUND IN
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Mu hammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} was asked about a man who was 
killed and whose body parts were found scattered.  
How should the funeral prayer be performed for him?  
Imam {a.s} said: "The prayer should be performed over the part that contains his heart." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.166 • Al -Wafi, Vol.24 p.491 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.3 
p.135

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 66, H. 5378

hadiths.chapter: 68. CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
hadiths.ar:
5380  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّسَّن ُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّلِى ِّ ن ْن ِ ق َّص  َّالٍ عَّن ِ إِن ْن ِ ن ُّكَّي ْزٍ عَّن ْ عُّن َّي ْذِ ن ْن ِ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ع لَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى   
إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي ُّو ْج َّذ ُّ وَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ جُّذُّوذ  إ ِجْذَّإهُّن  َّ إَّلْق َّي ْلُّ ق َّال مُّ ي ُّق ِي مُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلْحُّذُّوذَّ ق َّن ْلُّ ي ُّم َّ ي َّق ْي ُّلُّهُّ وَّ لأَّ 
ي ُّح َّالِف ْ عَّلِي  اً عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Faddal narrated from Zharif ibn Nasih, from Aban ibn 
Uthman, from Abu Basir, who narrated from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -
Baqir {a.s} regarding a man who intentionally killed another man and then fled, and he 
could not be captured until he died.  
Imam {a.s} said: "If he had wealth, the blood money is taken from it. If he had no wealth, 
it is taken from his closest relatives, and then from those next closest to him."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.167 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.10 p.170 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.4 
p.262 • Nuzhat Al -Nazir, Vol.1 p.132 • Awali Al -La’ali, Vol.2 p.365 • Awali Al -La’ali, Vol.3 p.604 • Al-Wafi, 
Vol.16 p.858 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.395  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 68, H. 5379

hadiths.chapter: 68. CHAPTER ON WHAT HAS BEEN REPORTED
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Faddal narrated from Ibn Bukayr, from Ubayd ibn Zurara, 
who narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s} regarding a man who was apprehended with 
multiple legal punishments (hudud) upon him, one of which was execution.  
Imam {a.s} said: "Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} used to carry out the other hudud first and 
then execute him. Do not oppose Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s}." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.250 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.167 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.10 
p.45 • Al-Wafi, Vol.15 p.545 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.28 p.34 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.28 p.35

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 68, H. 5380

hadiths.chapter: 69. CHAPTER ON THE BLOOD MONEY FOR WOUNDS AND
hadiths.ar:
5382  -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ إِن ْن ِ إَّلْمُّع ِي زَّة ِ عَّن ْ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ِ سِن َّان ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال « :
مِن َّ إَّلْأ ِن ِلِ
hadiths.en:
Al-Qasim ibn Muhammad Al-Jawhari narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah, from 
Abu Basir, who narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said: 
"For an Al-muḍihah (a wound that exposes the bone), the compensation is five camels.  
For the as -simhaq (a wound just below the skin but not exposing the bone), it is four 
camels.  
For the Al-munaqqilah (a wound that causes bone fragments), compensation is fifteen 
camels.  
For the Al -jaʾifah (a wound that reaches the internal organs), it is one -third of the full 
blood money, which is thirty-three camels.  
Likewise, for the Al -maʾmumah (a wound reaching the membrane of the brain), it is 
also one-third of the full blood money." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.326 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.167 • Ma’ani Al-Akhbar, Vol.1 p.329 
• Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.10 p.289 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.10 p.290 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.10 p.290 • Al-
Wafi, Vol.16 p.720 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.721 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.379 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.380  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 69, H. 5381

hadiths.chapter: 69. CHAPTER ON THE BLOOD MONEY FOR WOUNDS AND
hadiths.ar:
5383  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّسَّن ُّ ن ْن ُّ مَّحْن ُّوب ٍ عَّن ْ صَّالِحِ ن ْن ِ رَّر ِي ن ٍ عَّن ْ ذ َّرِي حٍ إَّلْمُّحَّارِن ِى ِّ ق َّالَّ: سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل هِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ 
عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ س َّح  َّ رَّج ُّلاً مُّوص ِحَّه ً وَّ س َّح  َّهُّ إ ج َّزُّ ذَّإمِي َّه ً ق ِى  مَّق َّامٍ وَّإجِذٍ ق َّمَّاب َّ إَّلز َّج ُّلُّ ق َّ ال 
ن ِصْق َّي ْن ِ
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Ibn Al -Mughirah from Abdullah ibn Sinan, from Abu 
Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}, that he said: 
"For the baḍiah (a wound that cuts through the flesh after the skin), the compensation 
is three camels." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.168 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.721  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 69, H. 5382
